library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى فتح الفيديو recording الجهاز "camera" داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic: القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه ( SDL_Camera *) Returns an SDL_Camera object or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل instance_id من النوع SDL_CameraID القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.

parameters.name: spec
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل spec من النوع const SDL_CameraSpec * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
the desired format for data the device will provide. Can be
NULL.

remarks:
You can open the device with any reasonable spec, and if the hardware
can't directly support it, it will convert data seamlessly to the
requested format. This might incur overhead, including scaling of image
data.

If you would rather accept whatever format the device offers, you can
pass a NULL spec here and it will choose one for you (and you can use SDL_Surface 's conversion/scaling functions
directly if necessary).

You can call SDL_GetCameraFormat () to get the
actual data format if passing a NULL spec here. You can see the exact
specs a device can support without conversion with SDL_GetCameraSupportedFormats ().

SDL will not attempt to emulate framerate; it will try to set the
hardware to the rate closest to the requested speed, but it won't
attempt to limit or duplicate frames artificially; call SDL_GetCameraFormat () to see the
actual framerate of the opened the device, and check your timestamps if
this is crucial to your app!

Note that the camera is not usable until the user approves its use!
On some platforms, the operating system will prompt the user to permit
access to the camera, and they can choose Yes or No at that point. Until
they do, the camera will not be usable. The app should either wait for
an SDL_EVENT_CAMERA_DEVICE_APPROVED
(or SDL_EVENT_CAMERA_DEVICE_DENIED )
event, or poll SDL_GetCameraPermissionState ()
occasionally until it returns non-zero. On platforms that don't require
explicit user approval (and perhaps in places where the user previously
permitted access), the approval event might come immediately, but it
might come seconds, minutes, or hours later!
details.returns: ( SDL_Camera *) Returns an SDL_Camera object or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
